## Langtidsplanen med satsing på luftvern

**Date:** Wed, 10 Apr 2024 21:35:53 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/10/langtidsplanen-med-satsing-pa-luftvern/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/10/langtidsplanen-med-satsing-pa-luftvern/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Prop. 87 S (2023–2024)</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Regjeringen ønsker å styrke forsvaret av Norge med 600 milliarder kroner de neste 12 årene, fra i 2024 til 2036.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Regjeringen ønsker at det kjøpes langtrekkende luftvern som skal beskytte Norge mot ballistiske missiler med kort rekkevidde i perioden 2028-2034. I tillegg at mengden dobles av det eksisterende NASAMS-luftvernet og at det skal utbedres for å beskytte bedre mot droner og missiler fra 2024 til 2036.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Luftvernsystemer som er donert til Ukraina ønskes erstattet. </p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Både Luftforsvaret og Hæren bør få flere systemer og dagens luftvernsystemer bør oppdateres.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2401,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5413-1.jpg?w=1024" alt="" class="wp-image-2401" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">NASAMS med økt volum og ytelse (2024-2036). Langtrekkende luftvern (2028-2034).</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Norge vil ta en lederrolle for å utvikle forsvarsteknologi innen luftvernsystemer og missiler slik at de også vil være relevante i fremtiden.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Det skal i 2024 opprettes et eget program i forsvarssektoren som skal ha helhetlig ansvar for at de samlede effektene av de ulike luftvern-prosjektene og utviklingsløpene realiseres.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Regjeringen kjøper langtrekkende luftvern som skal beskytte Norge mot ballistiske missiler med kort rekkevidde (for ett geografisk område).</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Luftvernsystemer som er donert til Ukraina skal erstattes.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Fire nye NASAMS-batterisystemer til Luftforsvaret og Hæren.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Det vil anskaffes nye radarer til NASAMS-systemet, i tråd med det som er tidligere vedtatt.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>I dag er det luftvernbataljoner på Ørlandet og Evenes. Her skal utholdenheten styrkes ved å øke bemanningen og øvingsnivået.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Finnmark landforsvar styrkes med kampluftvern.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2399,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5412.jpg?w=560" alt="" class="wp-image-2399" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">Regjeringen kjøper langtrekkende luftvern og dobler mengden med NASAMS-luftvern. Her er NASAMS avbildet i forbindelse med øvelsen Formidable Shield 2023. Foto: Martin Mellquist / Forsvaret</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><a href="https://www.regjeringen.no/no/dokumenter/prop.-87-s-20232024/id3032217/?fbclid=IwAR0a9mnZAfljp3unY5Bc_Oow5AOjjjmFUvOzeSU6Nn637MJChj3-FVkLskg_aem_AUMQ2AuzWLlSlkOZKxsWRPlDYFJU-zXLvwiSIpSTuLoPspZP8n3YNovrcT89aMkcHBY" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Prop. 87 S (2023–2024)</a></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><a href="https://www.regjeringen.no/no/dokumenter/kortversjon-av-forsvarsloftet-ny-langtidsplan-for-forsvarssektoren/id3032809/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Forsvarsløftet – for Norges trygghet</a></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><a href="https://www.regjeringen.no/no/aktuelt/pressekonferanse-om-langtidsplanen-for-forsvaret/id3032149/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Pressekonferanse om langtidsplanen for forsvaret</a></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

---

## Polens luftvernsatsning i 2024

**Date:** Mon, 15 Apr 2024 22:20:48 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/16/polens-luftvernsatsning-i-2024/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/16/polens-luftvernsatsning-i-2024/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Polen signerte i september 2023 kontrakter med europeiske og amerikanske selskaper for å styrke landets luftforsvar. Landet planlegger å bruke 137 milliarder zloty (32,9 milliarder dollar) i 2024, tilsvarende 4% av BNP.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Polen betaler for alt det nye utstyret gjennom en ekstra økning i forsvarsutgifter, sa Polens president Andrzej Duda under kontraktsigneringen på den første dagen av MSPO International Defence Industry Exhibition i Kielce, Polen, 5. september 2023.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Polen planlegger å kjøpe åtte Patriot-batterier og 22 Narew-batterier utstyrt med CAMM-ER missiler.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Landet har også inngått avtale om Pilica+ punktforsvarssystem med både standard CAMM-missiler og polskproduserte løsninger.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>I tillegg til å kjøpe eksisterende systemer samarbeider Polen med Storbritannia for å utvikle en enda lengre-rekkevidde missil kalt CAMM-MR.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2411,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5424-1.png?w=640" alt="" class="wp-image-2411" /></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Polen har allerede tatt imot to Patriot-batterier i fase 1 av Wisla-programmet. En kontrakt verdt 4.57 milliarder dollar.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Leveranser av elementene for neste fase av Wisla-programmet skal begynne i 2026 og være ferdig i 2029, og består blant annet av ytterligere seks Patriot-batterier.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Den siste Patriot-ordren inkluderer levering av 12 360 grader Lower-Tier luft- og missilforsvarsradarer, noe som gjør Polen til den første eksportkunden, samt 48 ekstra launchere og PAC-3-missiler.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Polen har ønsket en 360-graders radar for Patriot, da landet står overfor potensielle missiltrusler fra Russlands Kaliningrad Oblast på landets nordlige grense samt Hviterussland og Russland direkte i øst. Den andre fasen av Wisla krever fortsatt involvering i Patriot-programmet av polsk industri, inkludert produksjon og leveranser av M903-utskytningsramper og komponenter til PAC-3-missilene.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Polen har også bestilt mer enn 1000 MBDA Common Anti-Air Modular Missiles—Extended Range (CAMM-ER) og tilhørende utskytere som en del av Narew-programmet for middelsrekkende luftvern.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>CAMM-ER missiler er nøkkelkomponenter i Narew-systemet. Polen har allerede en variant av Narew i tjeneste, kalt Mala-Narew, som bruker CAMM-missiler.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2413,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5426-1.jpg?w=1024" alt="" class="wp-image-2413" /></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Det samme våpenet er også tatt i bruk for kortdistansekravet Pilica+, som det ble signert kontrakter for i april. Mala-Narew ble utviklet raskt for det polske militæret som svar på et presserende luftvernbehov. Men det endelige Narew-systemet vil kombinere CAMM-ER-er med det lokalt utviklede Pit-Radwar Sajna-radarsystemet. CAMM-ER har et forlenget og forstørret skrog, og kan nå mål ut til 40 km (25 mi.).</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2414,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5425-1.jpg?w=1024" alt="" class="wp-image-2414" /></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>I tillegg til missilene forventer Polen teknologioverføring for produksjon innenlands av missilene og deres launchere samt trenings- og logistikkpakker.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Leveranser av Narew-systemelementene vil pågå fra 2027-2035.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Missilprodusenten MBDA sier i en uttalelse på sosiale medier at de ønsker signeringen velkommen og ser fremover til å samarbeide med den polske forsvarsgruppen PGZ om prosjektet.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Både Narew og Wisla erstatter utdaterte systemer fra sovjettiden.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>"Dette muliggjør faktisk en veldig dynamisk modernisering av den polske hæren i mange sektorer samtidig," sa Duda. "Denne prosessen skjer og blir implementert … det virket kaotisk til å begynne med, men i dag ser vi de første effektene."</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

---

## Patriots in Norway and Sweden

**Date:** Mon, 22 Apr 2024 19:50:37 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/22/patriots-in-norway-and-sweden/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/22/patriots-in-norway-and-sweden/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In 1988, one could read in Stortingsmelding 58, approved on 3 June, that there was only one western missile system that could fully replace the area-wide NIKE system in terms of altitude coverage and range. The Patriot system. But that this would entail costs far in excess of what would be covered within the investment framework in question. No systems were thus acquired. </p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2428,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5441.jpg?w=1024" alt="" class="wp-image-2428" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">Area air defense for Eastern Norway with the NIKE system 1960-1991. Facsimile Luftled no. 2 June 2017.</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In 2016, long-range air defense was brought up to date again in Norway. According to the Long-Term Proposal (LTP, June), there was an ambition for procurement in the period 2022-2025. The new system was to be integrated with NASAMS II, and it was considered important that a modernized, developed and robust air defense structure was established when the F-35 aircraft are fully operational in 2025 at Ørland and Evenes air station.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:quote -->
<blockquote class="wp-block-quote"><!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Procurement of new long-range air defense is carried out in the period 2021–2024… It is important that a modernized, developed and robust air defense structure is established when the F-35 aircraft are fully operational in 2025 at Ørland and Evenes air station</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph --><cite>– LTP, 2016</cite></blockquote>
<!-- /wp:quote -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In March 2017, Sweden expressed a desire to start phasing in new air defense by 2020, and in the final phase of the tender process it was between the American Patriot and the European SAMP/T. The investment was initially estimated in the media at NOK 10-20 billion. In October, the suggestion was 30 billion. The American manufacturer claimed in 2017 to be able to deliver within the short deadline. A close industrial collaboration with Saab was also promised.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In Norway, the Ministry of Defense's annual overview of possible and planned materiel investments was published in April 2017. Here, the mentioned ambition for long-range air defense from the LTP had the status of "possible project", with a cost estimate of 3-5 billion. The purpose of the procurement was to improve the capability for protection against modern weapon threats carried out through the air, and includes new sensors and missiles that add today's air defense capability to long-range protection.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In November 2017, there was a press release from the Swedish Armed Forces that the choice had fallen on Patriot, with a renewed desire for phasing in from 2020 and with final deliveries by 2025. The Swedish authorities thus started negotiations with the manufacturer. At this time the extent of the trade was unclear, but initially at least one battery), with possible expansion to four.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Back in Norway, Parliamentary Bill 14S from 16 October 2020 stated that the existing NASAMS must be prioritized with new sensors and short-range missiles, rather than long-range air defenses, and the priority is explained by the fact that cruise missiles constitute the most demanding air threat for the country. While investment funds for long-range air defense were postponed until after 2028</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:quote -->
<blockquote class="wp-block-quote"><!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>… the current NASAMS systems will be upgraded from 2023 to 2029 with, among other things, modern sensors and short-range missiles. This is a necessary priority ahead of the planned introduction of long-range air defenses that was discussed in the previous long-term plan.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph --></blockquote>
<!-- /wp:quote -->

<!-- wp:quote -->
<blockquote class="wp-block-quote"><!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>… cruise missiles constitute the most demanding air threat for Norway, and NASAMS constitutes an important element in the active countermeasures</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph --><cite>- Parliamentary Bill 14S, 2020</cite></blockquote>
<!-- /wp:quote -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In 2018, the Swedish authorities chose to acquire four batteries with Patriot. In December 2022, approximately four years after the order was placed and two years ahead of schedule, the last battery arrived in the country.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2429,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5442-1.jpg?w=612" alt="" class="wp-image-2429" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">Patriot. Schematic layout for four batteries under one management/staff (Martin Ek/FMV)</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

---

## David's Sling air and missile defense system

**Date:** Tue, 23 Apr 2024 19:14:47 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/23/davids-sling-air-and-missile-defense-system/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/23/davids-sling-air-and-missile-defense-system/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Finland has decided to acquire the David's Sling long-range air defense manufactured by Israel (Rafael) and the USA (Raytheon). Cost set at at least 316 million euros. With the option of doubling.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>David's Sling air defense system is designed to be able to intercept missiles and large rockets within a range of 40 to 300 kilometers and can intercept missiles with a speed of Mach 7.5 at an altitude of 15 km and above. The system is equipped with the EL/M-2084 multifunction radar which is capable of detecting and following targets at distances of up to 250-300 km.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2444,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5447.jpg?w=800" class="wp-image-2444" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">EL/M-2084 multifunction radar (US MDA)</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The SkyCeptor missile intercepts tactical ballistic missiles and larger-caliber rockets at altitudes that reduce the possibility of damage from debris. The missile's high speed, precise guidance and the hit-to-kill principle ensure a good effect against a wide range of current and future air and missile threats. SkyCeptor's operational concept requires minimal cueing from sensor resources to ensure optimal firing rate - even during saturation attacks. SkyCeptor's modern design is adapted to the network-centric command and control systems of the future, at a reasonable price.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

---

## EMADS hits the mark?

**Date:** Wed, 24 Apr 2024 20:11:22 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/24/emads-hits-the-mark/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/24/emads-hits-the-mark/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The EMADS system, not a classic "off-the-shelf system" with everything you need in a box, but rather an idea, where the launch platform with missiles is the only clear common denominator, and the rest seems to be up to the buyer to specify - and for MBDA to integrate.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2453,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5452-1.jpg?w=709" class="wp-image-2453" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">EMADS (MBDA)<br>(Enhanced Modular Air Defense Solutions)</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The system consists of vertically fired active radar missiles, in two variants, with a range of up to 45 km. Based on the air-to-air missile ASRAAM, which with this system is fired from a road mobile platform. There is a two-way data link between the platform and the missile, which will ensure precise flight until the missile's radar can take over, and with the possibility that the operator can interrupt the engagement en route.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2454,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5453-1.jpg?w=256" class="wp-image-2454" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">CAMM / CAMM-ER (MBDA)<br>(Common anti-air modular missile / Extended range)</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>EMADS is presented as a modular system with great firepower and a high rate of fire. The manufacturer says the missile is not dependent on a specific type of multifunction radar, but can be integrated with any surveillance radar and has been developed with third-party insertion in mind, we are to believe the advertising.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:embed {"url":"https://youtu.be/nMWsLffI8Xk","type":"video","providerNameSlug":"youtube","responsive":true,"className":"wp-embed-aspect-16-9 wp-has-aspect-ratio"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-embed is-type-video is-provider-youtube wp-block-embed-youtube wp-embed-aspect-16-9 wp-has-aspect-ratio"><div class="wp-block-embed__wrapper">
https://youtu.be/nMWsLffI8Xk
</div></figure>
<!-- /wp:embed -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Furthermore, the choice of sensor(s) is apparently up to the buyer. To emphasize the flexibility of the system, MBDA leaves it up to the buyer to decide how many missiles a platform should be able to carry, but pictures from the manufacturer show platforms with 8-12 missiles.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2455,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5454-1.jpg?w=900" class="wp-image-2455" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">CAMM-ER (MBDA)</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The market will then have to show whether such an open approach hits the spot. So far, it is at least clear that the UK is buying CAMM when they are going to replace their Rapier FSC, while Italy is going for CAMM-ER to replace the SPADA and ASPIDE systems.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:embed {"url":"https://youtu.be/Of46e0Y7Kng","type":"video","providerNameSlug":"youtube","responsive":true,"className":"wp-embed-aspect-16-9 wp-has-aspect-ratio"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-embed is-type-video is-provider-youtube wp-block-embed-youtube wp-embed-aspect-16-9 wp-has-aspect-ratio"><div class="wp-block-embed__wrapper">
https://youtu.be/Of46e0Y7Kng
</div></figure>
<!-- /wp:embed -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>For the record; the maritime variant of the system MBDA calls Sea Ceptor.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:embed {"url":"https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=H50SvUpINEs","type":"video","providerNameSlug":"youtube","responsive":true,"className":"wp-embed-aspect-16-9 wp-has-aspect-ratio"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-embed is-type-video is-provider-youtube wp-block-embed-youtube wp-embed-aspect-16-9 wp-has-aspect-ratio"><div class="wp-block-embed__wrapper">
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=H50SvUpINEs
</div></figure>
<!-- /wp:embed -->

---

## Patriot and other Long-Range Air and Missile Defenses: What are The Benefits and The Downsides of Such Costly Systems?

**Date:** Sun, 28 Apr 2024 20:12:07 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/28/long-range-air-and-missile-defenses-on-the-horizon/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/28/long-range-air-and-missile-defenses-on-the-horizon/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>There are 18 nations worldwide that have or are in the process of acquiring Patriot today, and the number of user countries in Europe has doubled in 5-6 years. I addition, in Europe both France and Italy have deployed the European counterpart to Patriot; the SAMP/T system. In the Nordics Finland is acquiring the Israeli/US made David’s Sling system, while Sweden is among the many Patriot-user countries, and Norway is still only considering this class of weapon system.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2465,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5442-1-1.jpg?w=612" alt="" class="wp-image-2465" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">Patriot. Schematic layout for four batteries under one management/staff (Martin Ek/FMV)</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In light of this, this blog post will try to answer the following questions.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>What are the core benefits of long-range air and missile defenses?</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>What are the core down-sides of such systems?</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>What compromises and trade-offs in the overall air defense of a country must potential buyers with limited resources make, if quite a large percentage of allocated budgets is to be spent on the long-range capability?</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Long-range air defense benefits</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Compared with medium- and short-range systems a modern long-range system often bring more benefit in scenarios with ballistic short to medium range missiles/rockets, high altitude/stand-off precision bombing and other stand-off platforms for intelligence, reconnaissance and surveillance. Weapons with ballistic profiles can be shot down, bombers are forced to fly around or under, and aircraft and drones with stand-off missions are kept even further away from the battlefield/airport/defended area. Furthermore, in order to accomplish this, modern long-range systems utilize sophisticated radar and missile technology significantly driving the cost per unit up. The most expensive system of this class is the Patriot air and missile defense system, where one battery with engagement radar and the latest set of hit-to-kill missiles amounts to more than one billion US dollars. This according to a December 16, 2022, Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS) article. </p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>The down-sides of long-range systems</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Long-range air defense systems are, without exception, dependent on radar to accomplish their mission. Evidently, in order to be able to intercept, the targets are required to fly within the coverage area of these systems’ radars. Any target that either is above or outside of the system radar coverage is consequently flying without impunity. However, a more subtle added feature is that this also apply to targets flying below what’s called the radar horizon. At range the earth’s curvature allow low flying targets to evade radar detection. So put simply, low flying targets can be as safe as targets at altitudes above or outside of maximum coverage.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Long-range air and missile defenses on the horizon</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>What distances and flight altitudes are we talking about? Example; an aircraft at 500 feet is below the horizon of a radar at a height of 5 m, until it is within a distance of approx. 60 km. A quadrupling of the radar’s height above ground in the same scenario unfortunately only gives a relatively small increase, barely 10 km extra, i.e. a theoretical detection range of 70 km for a target at 500 feet.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:quote -->
<blockquote class="wp-block-quote"><!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Targets are just as safe below the radar horizon as they are at altitudes above maximum air defense coverage.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph --><cite>norskluftvern.com</cite></blockquote>
<!-- /wp:quote -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>If the engagement radar of a long-range system can see targets out to 150 km, and the missile reach is 75% of this, we realize that a typical long-range system is not particularly more long-range than other systems at lower altitudes, simply due to the limitations from the radar horizon.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2462,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5465-1.jpg?w=668" alt="" class="wp-image-2462" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">The figure published by basicsaboutaerodynamicsandavionics.com shows how low level coverage for ground radars is limited by the principle of the radar horizon.</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Elevated sensors can be a measure to reduce challenges with detection and target tracking in a scenario with low-flying targets. Compared to sensors in aircraft, balloons with sensors and connections have a built-in ability to maintain higher air surveillance readiness over a longer period of time.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The American program to develop an aerostat "JLENS" realized this a long time ago. However, since its inception, which is at least since 1998, this aerostat project has been plagued by technological challenges, budget cuts, and accidents during field testing.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2467,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5466-1.jpg?w=808" alt="" class="wp-image-2467" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">A JLENS aerostat is launched at White Sands Missile Range. The aerostat is equipped with a powerful radar.</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>The arguments for an alternative approach</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>An alternative approach to the costly long-range systems, is the lighter, less sophisticated classes of medium ranged ground based air defense missile systems. These are more affordable systems, that could be acquired in larger numbers. Thus enabling point air defense of a greater number of objects against low altitude targets, as compared to what a relatively few heavy long-range systems could do.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>It is fair to underline here that there are target sets that only the modern long-range systems can defend against, and leaving the door open so to speak in this respect, does not come without risk. </p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>However, it is this blog’s opinion that a balanced approach to the air defense problem should take the following points into consideration:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>1) A distributed network of mobile and fixed passive and active sensors:</strong>&nbsp;including sensors on land, on ships, on satellites, and on drones.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>2) Acquisition of more and affordable short-range and medium-range air defense and missile defense systems.</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>3) High-energy weapons, laser cannons, and jammers around important targets as an additional line of defense in addition to classic point defense air defense.</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>4) Robust command and control systems that can aggregate data from many systems, including those of allies, and ensure effective use of air defense and missile defense.</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>5) Passive defense of forces and facilities:</strong> including camouflage, concealment, and deception measures, as well as fortification and the ability to reorganize and repair to make it difficult for the enemy to choose targets and to ensure that one’s own forces are able to maintain combat effectiveness throughout the course of the battle.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>These measures would create a robust foundation for a bread &amp; butter ground based air defense capacity, in which a more sophisticated and expensive and thinner layer of long-range and missile defense capability could contribute. </p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

---

## MEADS what happened?

**Date:** Tue, 30 Apr 2024 13:36:17 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/30/meads-what-happened/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/30/meads-what-happened/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong><em>The story of a Patriot that would not go away, and Europe that partly went it alone</em></strong>.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>- <strong><em>How a multinational approach failed.</em></strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The system was intended as a replacement for the Patriot, and supposed to go with the PAC-3 MSE as its primary missile, although plans were made for multiple missile types as well.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The most obvious capability difference between Patriot and MEADS was that the latter's radar provides 360 degree coverage and supports the full capability of the MSE missile.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In 1999, MEADS International* was selected by NATO MEADS Management Agency** to develop MEADS. Participating companies were MBDA Italia, the European Aeronautic Defence and Space Company (EADS), Lenkflugkorpersysteme (LFK) in Germany and Lockheed Martin in the United States.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Together these companies focused an international engineering team in Orlando to develop systems and technologies for the MEADS program.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In September 2004, the NAMEADSMA awarded MEADS International a letter contract valued at approximately $2B plus £1.4B to design and develop the system. </p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>However, the story of MEADS has so far has resulted in no sales. Neither the USA, Germany, Italy nor any other major European country have acquired the system. Instead, the US has embarked on a process of gradually updating Patriot, inspired by the experience of developing MEADS. Back in Europe Italy teamed up with France for the Eurosam joint venture, and today both countries successfully deploy the SAMP/T air and missile defense system with the vertical launched ASTER missiles.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>An upgraded version named SAMP/T NG,  is underway and is due for serial production in 2025. The next generation SAMP/T comes with a new hit-to-kill missile and an AESA radar with 360 degrees coverage.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2488,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/image.jpg?w=569" alt="" class="wp-image-2488" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">SAMP/T NG AESA radar. Image: Eurosam</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2491,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5479-1.jpg?w=540" alt="" class="wp-image-2491" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">SAMP/T NG truck mounted vertical launcher for ASTER missiles. Image: Eurosam</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In 2022 Germany turned its attention the next generation Patriot with a developmental 360 degrees radar called GhostEye, which together with PAC-3 missiles formes the new system also known as Lower Tier Air and Missile Defense System (LTAMDS).&nbsp;This system is intended to ensure a 360° firing sector against cruise missiles, drones and ballistic battlefield weapons, as well as as a terminal defense together with AEGIS and THAAD.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2479,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5451.jpg?w=1024" alt="" class="wp-image-2479" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">Raytheon GhostEye AESA radar with 360° coverage. Originally produced for the next generation of long-range anti-aircraft. Image: Raytheon.</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Patriot is currently sold to, or in use by, as many as 18 countries worldwide, and the market for these new upgrades of Patriot is thus significant.<br />In Europe, the users are the Netherlands (1987), Germany (1989), Greece (1999), Spain (2004), Romania (2017), Sweden (2018), Poland (2018) and Switzerland (2022). In the rest of the world, there are a further nine user countries: Japan, Israel, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Taiwan, Korea, United Arab Emirates, Qatar and Bahrain. Sorted by acquisition date.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>*NAMEADSMA was a chartered organization of NATO. </p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>**A multinational joint venture headquartered in Orlando, FL.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

---

## The European Patriot - SAMP/T Air and Missile Defense System

**Date:** Wed, 01 May 2024 18:54:34 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/05/01/samp-t-the-european-patriot/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/05/01/samp-t-the-european-patriot/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The SAMP/T air defense system has emerged as Europe’s flagship alternative to the U.S.-made Patriot, offering advanced capabilities while reinforcing continental defense sovereignty. Developed through Franco-Italian collaboration and continuously modernized, this system addresses evolving threats through cutting-edge technology and strategic interoperability.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The SAMP/T (Sol-Air Moyenne Portée Terrestre) originated from a joint effort between France and Italy under Eurosam, a consortium formed by MBDA Missile Systems and Thales.&nbsp;Initially deployed in 2010, its design focused on countering tactical ballistic missiles (up to 600 km range), cruise missiles, and aircraft.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>SAMP/T, with Aster-15 and Aster-30 block 1 missiles, can launch to 30 and 120 km against aerodynamic profiles. The Aster-30 has a maximum speed of M4.5 and is capable of countering tactical ballistic missiles with a range of up to 600 km.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Development began in earnest in 1990, with testing of individual components from 1999 and with the entire system from 2005.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2510,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/img_5403.jpg?w=1024" alt="" class="wp-image-2510" /></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph {"className":"my-0"} -->
<p class="my-0">Operational since 2010, SAMP/T systems are fielded by France, Italy, and Ukraine:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list {"className":"marker:text-textOff list-disc"} -->
<ul class="wp-block-list marker:text-textOff list-disc"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>In 2010 the France </strong>acquired three batteries for its army and seven for the air force. In France the system goes under the MAMBA name. Italy had by 2012 received five batteries. </li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Aster maritime application.</strong> Italy, France and Great Britain also operate the Aster-30 as maritime air defense and missile defense, and these systems are respectively called PAAMS (Fr/It) and Sea Viper.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>In March 2017, Sweden</strong> expressed a desire to start phasing in a new air defense system by 2020, and in the final phase of the tender process it was between the American Patriot and the European SAMP/T. The Swedes chose Patriot. In June 2021 Switzerland also chose Patriot over SAMP/T.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Accelerated missile production. </strong>France and Italy accelerated Aster missile production in 2025 amid U.S. aid uncertainties, prioritizing supply chain security.&nbsp;Denmark’s pending choice between SAMP/T NG and Patriot reflects growing European defense autonomy.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Ukraine’s deployment&nbsp;</strong>demonstrated combat efficacy, with a SAMP/T battery intercepting a Russian jet at 90-mile ranges in March 2025.&nbsp;Each $500 million battery can fire 8 missiles in 10 seconds.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Cost. </strong>SAMP/T as compared to Patriot seems like a more affordable system; where a full battery with Patriot costs from $600 to 1000 million, you may get SAMP/T for half the money.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>A nominal battery</strong> consists of a passive phase-guided (PESA) X-band multifunction radar with 360° coverage, up to six launch pads with eight missiles, which can be deployed up to 10 km away from the radar. Such a fire unit can track more than 100 targets and engage 10 of them simultaneously. The main components of the system can be transported by C-130J and A400M transport aircraft.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2511,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/img_5404-1.jpg?w=638" alt="" class="wp-image-2511" /></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Modernization. </strong>An upgraded version named SAMP/T NG, is underway and is due for serial production in 2025. The next generation SAMP/T comes with hit-to-kill missiles and an AESA radar with 360 degrees coverage.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The Aster-30 (called block 1 NT for "New Technology"). The missile gets a new radar with a higher frequency (Ka-band radar, against today's "block 1" which uses Ku-band) for more gain/narrower lobe and with e.g. greater accuracy and resolution, and with a greater probability of direct hits.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2498,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/img_5468.jpg?w=1024" alt="" class="wp-image-2498" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">SAMP/T Image: Eurosam</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:list {"className":"marker:text-textOff list-disc"} -->
<ul class="wp-block-list marker:text-textOff list-disc"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Aster 30 Block 1NT missiles</strong>&nbsp;with upgraded seekers and computers to engage hypersonic threats and medium-range ballistic missiles (up to 1,500 km)<a class="citation ml-xs inline" href="https://finabel.org/italian-french-cooperation-for-the-development-of-a-new-air-defence-system/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener"></a><a class="citation ml-xs inline" href="https://armyrecognition.com/news/army-news/army-news-2024/france-to-receive-new-generation-of-samp-t-ng-air-defense-missile-system-by-2026" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener"></a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>AESA radars</strong>&nbsp;(Ground Fire 300 or Kronos Grand Mobile) providing 360° coverage and detection ranges exceeding 350 km<a class="citation ml-xs inline" href="https://armyrecognition.com/news/army-news/2025/samp-t-ng-air-defense-system-at-the-core-of-european-air-defense-with-first-gf300-mri-radar-validated" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener"></a><a class="citation ml-xs inline" href="https://defence-industry.eu/samp-t-ng-a-new-european-system-for-the-multi-layered-air-defence/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener"></a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Modular architecture</strong>&nbsp;enabling integration with SHORAD systems like VL MICA and CAMM-ER for multi-layered defense<a class="citation ml-xs inline" href="https://defence-industry.eu/samp-t-ng-a-new-european-system-for-the-multi-layered-air-defence/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener"></a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph {"className":"my-0"} -->
<p class="my-0">This €4 billion modernization effort, managed by OCCAR, emphasizes rapid deployment, reduced crew requirements, and compatibility with NATO networks<a class=" mr-[2px] citation ml-xs inline" href="https://www.mbda-systems.com/products/area-protection/aster-family/aster-sampt-ng" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener"></a></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2500,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/img_5483-1.jpg?w=570" alt="" class="wp-image-2500" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">SAMP/T NG AESA radar Image: Eurosam</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2499,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/img_5480-1.jpg?w=659" alt="" class="wp-image-2499" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">SAMP/T NG interior Image: Eurosam</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2501,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/img_5479-1.jpg?w=540" alt="" class="wp-image-2501" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">SAMP/T NG launcher Image: Eurosam</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2505,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/img_5477-1.png?w=390" alt="" class="wp-image-2505" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">SAMP/T NG key figures Image: Eurosam</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

## Sveits anskaffer mer luftvern

**Date:** Mon, 13 May 2024 21:54:14 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/05/13/sveits-anskaffer-mer-luftvern/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/05/13/sveits-anskaffer-mer-luftvern/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Den 30. april 2024 utstedte det sveitsiske føderale kontoret for forsvarsanskaffelser, Armasuisse, offisielt en forespørsel om forslag til tre produsenter for anskaffelse av nye bakkebaserte luftvernsystemer med middels rekkevidde.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Prosjektet går under navnet Bodluv MR, og beslutning om anskaffes er planlagt i tredje kvartal 2024. <a href="https://www.vbs.admin.ch/de/nsb?id=100856" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Les mer her</a> </p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Armasuisse har fire potensielle systemer fra tre leverandører:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>- IRIS-T SLM fra Diehl Defence (Tyskland),<br />- NASAMS fra Kongsberg og Raytheon (USA), og<br />- <a href="https://norskluftvern.com/2024/05/01/samp-t-the-european-patriot/">SAMP/T</a> og <a href="https://norskluftvern.com/2024/04/24/emads-hits-the-mark/">EMADS</a> fra MBDA (Frankrike).</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>I juni 2021 besluttet Sveits å gå til anskaffelse av fem batterier med Patriot langtrekkende luftvern. I november 2021 fremkom det at kontrakten for Patriot pålydende rundt 2,1 milliarder amerikanske dollar. I september 2022 vedtok det sveitsiske parlamentet at anskaffelsene kunne iverksettes. I februar 2023 ble det kjent at Sveits ville utvide Patriot-kontrakten med drøyt 320 millioner dollar til å inkludere produsentens PAC-3 MSE missiler, slik at systemet også mer effektivt kan skyte ned taktiske ballistiske missiler. <a href="https://norskluftvern.com/2023/02/22/sveitsisk-patriot/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Les mer her</a></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

---

## What does the Patriot Air and Missile Defense System cost the Romanians?

**Date:** Sat, 15 Jun 2024 19:59:13 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/06/15/romanian-patriots/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/06/15/romanian-patriots/)

<!-- wp:heading {"className":""} -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>A Detailed Look at the Missile Defense System Acquisition</strong></h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Introduction</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In July 2017,&nbsp;the United States government approved a potential sale of seven Patriot missile batteries to Romania for $3.9 billion.&nbsp;The following year,&nbsp;a supplementary agreement was reached between the manufacturer and Romania for the purchase of three additional batteries.&nbsp;The total cost is undisclosed.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Key Points</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Romania is acquiring a total of ten Patriot missile batteries.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>The batteries will be divided between the Romanian Air Force and Army.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>The first four batteries were likely delivered in 2022.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Romania has also signed an agreement for the licensed production of SkyCeptor missiles,&nbsp;a more affordable alternative to Patriot missiles.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Additional Details</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>The Patriot missile system is an air defense system designed to intercept and destroy tactical ballistic missiles,cruise missiles,&nbsp;and other airborne threats.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>The PAC-2 GEM-T and PAC-3 MSE are the two types of Patriot missiles that Romania will be acquiring.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>The SkyCeptor missile is based on the Stunner missile from the Israeli-American David's Sling system.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>The sale in total potentially includes 10 AN/MPQ-65 radars,&nbsp;10 AN/MSQ-132 control vehicles,&nbsp;40 M903 launchers,&nbsp;80 MIM-104E (GEM-T) missiles,&nbsp;240 PAC-3 MSE missiles,&nbsp;10 generators,&nbsp;and communication antennas.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Significance</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The acquisition of Patriot missile batteries is a significant step forward for Romania's air defense capabilities.&nbsp;The system will provide Romania with the ability to defend itself against a wider range of threats,&nbsp;including tactical ballistic missiles.The licensed production of SkyCeptor missiles will also help to reduce Romania's reliance on foreign suppliers for critical defense equipment.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Conclusion</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The Romanian Patriot missile acquisition is a major investment in the country's defense.&nbsp;The system will help to protect Romania from a variety of airborne threats and will also boost the country's defense industry.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

---

## Om langtrekkende luftvern

**Date:** Wed, 19 Jun 2024 18:09:37 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/06/19/om-langtrekkende-luftvern/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/06/19/om-langtrekkende-luftvern/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Den offentlige debatten om hvor mye luftvern, med hvilke kvaliteter, Norge egentlig trenger pågår. Det er imidlertid nødvendig å kvalifisere størrelsene som ofte går igjen i diskusjonene.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Skal Norge ha langtrekkende luftvern som kan skyte ned tradisjonelle bemannede og ubemannede fly og kryssermissiler på lange avstander? Eller er det et forsvar mot taktiske ballistiske missiler som er den drivende faktoren? Dette er ikke to sider av samme sak dessverre. Offisielle dokumenter tyder på det siste, men i den åpne diskursen er det som oftest begrepet «langtrekkende luftvern» som går igjen.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Skal Norge ha et TROVERDIG forsvar mot taktiske ballistiske missiler? Mon det er ytterligere en annen sak.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2465,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/img_5442-1-1.jpg?w=612" alt="" class="wp-image-2465" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">Patriot. Skjematisk oppsett for fire systemer (batterier) under en ledelse/stab (bataljon) (Martin Ek/FMV)</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Enn så lenge er det dessverre slik at omfanget «langtrekkende luftvern» som det nå snakkes om, to eller kanskje tre systemer, uansett i hovedsak betyr en symbolsk evne, all den tid disse få systemene er tenkt utplassert svært spredt for å dekke to nasjonalt viktige punkter på kartet. For det er faktisk slik at denne klassen av systemer er nødt til å utgruppere svært tett på objektet som skal beskyttes, dersom det er ønskelig å sikre en høy grad av treffsannsynlighet. Og her kommer noe sentralt å få med seg. Denne typen luftverndekning har ingenting med lange rekkevidder å gjøre, nedskyting av taktiske ballistiske missiler vil skje ganske så tett på objektet. I tillegg vil utplassering av kun ett system per beskyttede objekt være lite robust og ganske så sårbart, spesielt dersom retningen hvorfra de angripende missilene kommer, ikke kan forutses tidlig nok. Dette fordi dagens systemer med evne mot denne typen våpen ikke dekker 360 grader av sirkelen, men man er avhengig av flere systemer per objekt man vil forsvare fra alle retninger samtidig. </p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2549,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/img_5824-1.jpg?w=917" alt="" class="wp-image-2549" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">Jfr. prinsipper for mengde/evne til styrkekonsentrasjon og retningslinjer for utgruppering av luftvern. Denne figuren fra en amerikansk feltmanual viser hva de doktrinelt tenkte rundt dette. Seks systemer relativt tett utgruppert for dekning av ett «område / objekt». (Fig. US DoD)</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Og ikke nok med det, dersom noen skulle komme til å sende offensive våpen inn mot våre objekter, så vil ikke dette kunne begrense seg til et par ballistiske missiler, men heller komme som en coctail av ulike våpentyper for å sikre at noen av dem kommer seg helt frem til målet, fordi forsvareren (vi) går i metning, og/eller ikke har dekning i alle sektorer og høydesjikt.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Dette, og mere til, er nok årsaken til at satsningen på luftvern i gjeldende langtidsplan blir karakterisert som «for lite, for sent og for smalt».</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Noen eksempler:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><a href="https://www.stratagem.no/la-oss-snakke-om-langtrekkende-luftvern/amp/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">La oss snakke om langtrekkende luftvern</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><a href="https://www.regjeringen.no/no/aktuelt/historisk-satsing-pa-luftvern-norge-anskaffer-langtrekkende-luftvern/id3032678/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Historisk satsning på luftvern</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><a href="https://www.forsvaretsforum.no/drone-droner-luftforsvaret/nytt-luftvern-for-lite-og-for-seint/352738" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Nytt luftvern - for lite, for seint</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><a href="https://www.stortinget.no/no/Hva-skjer-pa-Stortinget/Nyhetsarkiv/Hva-skjer-nyheter/20232024/stortingsdebatt-om-langtidsplan-for-forsvaret/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Stortingsdebatt om langtidsplan for Forsvaret</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><a href="https://www.forsvaretsforum.no/ine-eriksen-soreide-langtidsplanen-luftforsvaret/mener-langtrekkende-luftvern-ikke-er-nok-til-a-beskytte-oslo/381387" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Langtrekkende luftvern ikke nok til å forsvare Oslo</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Les mer om hva som trengs for et robust luftvern- og missilforsvar på www.norskluftvern.com</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:embed {"url":"https://www.forsvarsforeningen.no/nyheter/siste-ord-er-ikke-sagt-om-beskyttelse-av-sivile-og-militaere-mal/?fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTEAAR0NbvNP41LPo7ipIGsHFsc_FM2MR-K7i32NcsGTfiVUHFzsdrJuDwVoT_s_aem_ZmFrZWR1bW15MTZieXRlcw","type":"wp-embed","providerNameSlug":"norges-forsvarsforening"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-embed is-type-wp-embed is-provider-norges-forsvarsforening wp-block-embed-norges-forsvarsforening"><div class="wp-block-embed__wrapper">
https://www.forsvarsforeningen.no/nyheter/siste-ord-er-ikke-sagt-om-beskyttelse-av-sivile-og-militaere-mal/?fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTEAAR0NbvNP41LPo7ipIGsHFsc_FM2MR-K7i32NcsGTfiVUHFzsdrJuDwVoT_s_aem_ZmFrZWR1bW15MTZieXRlcw
</div></figure>
<!-- /wp:embed -->

## Switzerland Bolsters Air Defense Capabilities with Medium-Range Systems

**Date:** Sat, 22 Jun 2024 11:47:28 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/06/22/switzerland-bolsters-air-defense-capabilities-with-medium-range-systems/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/06/22/switzerland-bolsters-air-defense-capabilities-with-medium-range-systems/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>On April 30, 2024, the Swiss Federal Office for Armaments Procurement, Armasuisse, officially issued a request for proposals (RFP) to three manufacturers for the acquisition of new medium-range ground-based air defense systems.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The project, named Bodluv MR, is scheduled for a decision on procurement in the third quarter of 2024.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Armasuisse has shortlisted four potential systems from three suppliers:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>IRIS-T SLM from Diehl Defence (Germany)</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>NASAMS from Kongsberg and Raytheon (USA)</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>SAMP/T and EMADS from MBDA (France)</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>This procurement follows Switzerland's decision in June 2021 to acquire five batteries of Patriot long-range air defense systems. In November 2021, the contract for the Patriot systems was valued at approximately 2.1 billion US dollars. The Swiss Parliament approved the procurement in September 2022. In February 2023, it was announced that Switzerland would expand the Patriot contract by over 320 million dollars to include the manufacturer's PAC-3 MSE missiles, enabling the system to more effectively engage tactical ballistic missiles.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Main points:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Switzerland is seeking to procure new medium-range ground-based air defense systems to enhance its air defense capabilities.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Four potential systems from three suppliers are under consideration.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>This procurement follows Switzerland's recent acquisition of Patriot long-range air defense systems.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>More information:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Armasuisse: <a class="traceable-link" href="https://www.ar.admin.ch/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">https://www.ar.admin.ch/</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>IRIS-T SLM: <a class="traceable-link" href="https://www.diehl.com/defence/en/products/air-defence-systems/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">https://www.diehl.com/defence/en/products/air-defence-systems/</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>NASAMS: <a class="traceable-link" href="//www.kongsberg.com/kda/what-we-do/defence-and-security/integrated-air-and-missile-defence/nasams-air-defence-system/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">https://www.kongsberg.com/kda/what-we-do/defence-and-security/integrated-air-and-missile-defence/nasams-air-defence-system/</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>SAMP/T and EMADS: <a class="traceable-link" href="https://www.mbda-systems.com/solutions-and-services/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">https://www.mbda-systems.com/solutions-and-services/</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Patriot air defense system: <a class="traceable-link" href="https://www.rtx.com/raytheon/what-we-do/integrated-air-and-missile-defense/global-patriot-solutions" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">https://www.rtx.com/raytheon/what-we-do/integrated-air-and-missile-defense/global-patriot-solutions</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

## Norway: Long-term plan with focus on air defense

**Date:** Sat, 22 Jun 2024 12:11:23 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/06/22/norway-long-term-plan-with-focus-on-air-defense/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/06/22/norway-long-term-plan-with-focus-on-air-defense/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Prop. 87 S (2023-2024)</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The Norwegian government proposes to strengthen the country's defense with 600 billion kroner over the next 12 years,from 2024 to 2036.&nbsp;This significant investment aims to enhance Norway's ability to protect itself against evolving threats in the modern security landscape.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Key takeaways:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong class="">Long-range air defense:</strong>&nbsp;Acquisition of long-range air defense systems to shield Norway against short-range ballistic missiles within the 2028-2034 timeframe.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Doubling NASAMS air defense:</strong>&nbsp;Doubling the quantity of the existing NASAMS air defense system and upgrading it to better counter drones and missiles from 2024 to 2036.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Replacing air defense systems donated to Ukraine:</strong>&nbsp;Replacing air defense systems donated to Ukraine to support their ongoing conflict.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Enhancing air defense capabilities for both Air Force and Army:</strong>&nbsp;Equipping both the Norwegian Air Force and Army with additional air defense systems and upgrading existing ones.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>NASAMS enhancements:</strong>&nbsp;Implementing volume and performance upgrades for NASAMS air defense systems (2024-2036).</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Leadership in air defense technology development:</strong>&nbsp;Taking a leading role in developing air defense system and missile technology to ensure future relevance.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Dedicated air defense program:</strong>&nbsp;Establishing a dedicated air defense program within the defense sector in 2024 to holistically manage the realization of the combined effects of various air defense projects and development initiatives.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Specific measures:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Long-range air defense procurement:</strong>&nbsp;The government will procure long-range air defense systems to protect Norway against short-range ballistic missiles (covering a specific geographical area).</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Replacing donated air defense systems:</strong>&nbsp;Replacing air defense systems donated to Ukraine.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Additional NASAMS batteries:</strong>&nbsp;Providing four new NASAMS battery systems to the Air Force and Army.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>New radars for NASAMS:</strong>&nbsp;Acquiring new radars for the NASAMS system,&nbsp;in line with previous decisions.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Strengthening air defense battalions:</strong>&nbsp;Strengthening the endurance of existing air defense battalions in Ørlandet and Evenes by increasing staffing and training levels.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Enhancing Finnmark land defense:</strong>&nbsp;Bolstering Finnmark land defense with combat air defense capabilities.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Overall, the long-term plan with a focus on air defense represents a comprehensive and substantial effort by the Norwegian government to safeguard the nation's security against emerging threats in the 21st century.</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Image: Martin Mellquist / Forsvaret</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

---

## Poland's Air Defense Investment in 2024

**Date:** Sat, 22 Jun 2024 12:24:04 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/06/22/polands-air-defense-investment-in-2024/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/06/22/polands-air-defense-investment-in-2024/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In September 2023, Poland signed contracts with European and American companies to strengthen the country's air defense. The country plans to spend 137 billion zloty (US$32.9 billion) in 2024, equivalent to 4% of GDP.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Poland is paying for all the new equipment through an extra increase in defense spending, Polish President Andrzej Duda said during the contract signing on the first day of the MSPO International Defence Industry Exhibition in Kielce, Poland, on September 5, 2023.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Poland plans to buy eight Patriot batteries and 22 Narew batteries equipped with CAMM-ER missiles.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The country has also signed a deal for the Pilica+ point defense system with both standard CAMM missiles and Polish-produced solutions.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In addition to buying existing systems, Poland is working with the UK to develop an even longer-range missile called CAMM-MR.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2559,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/img_5836-1.png?w=640" class="wp-image-2559" /></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Poland has already received two Patriot batteries in phase 1 of the Wisla program. A contract worth $4.57 billion.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Deliveries of elements for the next phase of the Wisla program are scheduled to begin in 2026 and be completed in 2029, and include, among other things, six additional Patriot batteries.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The latest Patriot order includes the delivery of 12 360-degree Lower-Tier Air and Missile Defense radars, making Poland the first export customer, as well as 48 additional launchers and PAC-3 missiles.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Poland has wanted a 360-degree radar for Patriot, as the country faces potential missile threats from Russia's Kaliningrad Oblast on its northern border as well as Belarus and Russia directly to the east. The second phase of Wisla still requires Polish industry involvement in the Patriot program, including the production and delivery of M903 launchers and components for the PAC-3 missiles.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Poland has also ordered more than 1,000 MBDA Common Anti-Air Modular Missiles—Extended Range (CAMM-ER) and associated launchers as part of the Narew program for medium-range air defense.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>CAMM-ER missiles are key components of the Narew system. Poland already has a variant of Narew in service, called Mala-Narew, which uses CAMM missiles.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2560,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/img_5837.jpg?w=1022" class="wp-image-2560" /></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The same weapon has also been adopted for the Pilica+ short-range requirement, for which contracts were signed in April. Mala-Narew was developed rapidly for the Polish military in response to an urgent air defense need. But the final Narew system will combine CAMM-ERs with the locally developed Pit-Radwar Sajna radar system. CAMM-ER has an extended and enlarged airframe and can engage targets up to 40 km (25 mi).</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2561,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/img_5838.jpg?w=1022" class="wp-image-2561" /></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In addition to the missiles, Poland expects technology transfer for domestic production of the missiles and their launchers, as well as training and logistics packages.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Deliveries of Narew system elements will take place from 2027-2035.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Missile manufacturer MBDA says in a social media statement that they welcome the signing and look forward to working with the Polish defense group PGZ on the project.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Both Narew and Wisla replace outdated Soviet-era systems.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>"This actually enables a very dynamic modernization of the Polish army in many sectors at the same time," Duda said. "This process is happening and being implemented... it seemed chaotic at first, but today we are seeing the first effects."</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

---

## Norge inngår kontrakt på kjøp av NASAMS-utstyr for inntil fire batterier

**Date:** Sat, 29 Jun 2024 19:15:47 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/06/29/norge-inngar-kontrakt-pa-kjop-av-nasams-utst-for-inntil-fire-batterier/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/06/29/norge-inngar-kontrakt-pa-kjop-av-nasams-utst-for-inntil-fire-batterier/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Norge har signert en kontrakt med Kongsberg Defence &amp; Aerospace (KDA) for å kjøpe mer NASAMS-luftvern til en verdi på 2,3 milliarder kroner, i tillegg til en fastprisopsjon på mer luftvern til 2,5 milliarder kroner.&nbsp;</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:image {"id":2617,"sizeSlug":"large"} -->
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img src="https://norskluftvern.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/img_5930-1.jpg?w=1024" alt="" class="wp-image-2617" /><figcaption class="wp-element-caption"><br />Luftvernbataljonen på Evenes gjennomfører skarpskyting med NASAMS under øvelse Sølvpil på Andøya. Foto: Fabian Helmersen / Forsvaret</figcaption></figure>
<!-- /wp:image -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Dette gjøres for å:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Styrke Norges luftvern mot angrep.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Erstatte materiell som er donert til Ukraina.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Legge til rette for fremtidige donasjoner.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Doble antall luftvernbatterier i Norge.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Avtalen inkluderer:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Bestilling av flere NASAMS-utskytningsenheter og ildledningssentraler.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Forhåndsbestilling av komponenter med lang ledetid.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>En opsjon på å kjøpe mer luftvern i fremtiden.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Fordelene med den nye kontrakten:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Raskere levering av luftvern til Forsvaret.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Økt produksjonskapasitet i norsk forsvarsindustri.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Norge får tilgang til den nyeste generasjonen av NASAMS.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Norge styrker sin evne til å forsvare seg mot lufttrusler.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Dette er en historisk satsing på norsk luftvern.</strong>&nbsp;Den vil bidra til å gjøre Norge tryggere og bedre rustet til å møte fremtidige trusler.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Noen viktige punkter:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Kontrakten er en utvidelse av en tidligere avtale mellom Forsvarsmateriell og KDA.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Avtalen sikrer at Norge får tilgang til NASAMS-komponenter med lang ledetid raskt.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>KDA har investert i å utvide sin produksjonskapasitet for å møte den økte etterspørselen.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Norge tar en lederrolle i utviklingen av luftvernteknologi.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><a href="https://www.fma.no/aktuelt-og-media/2024/ny-kontrakt-sikrer-rask-tilgang-til-nytt-luftvern" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Link til FMA pressemelding </a></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

## Norway Signs Contract to Purchase NASAMS Equipment for Up to Four Batteries

**Date:** Sat, 29 Jun 2024 19:36:59 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2024/06/29/norway-signs-contract-to-purchase-nasams-equipment-for-up-to-four-batteries/](http://norskluftvern.com/2024/06/29/norway-signs-contract-to-purchase-nasams-equipment-for-up-to-four-batteries/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Norway has signed a contract with Kongsberg Defence &amp; Aerospace (KDA) to buy more NASAMS air defense systems worth NOK 2.3 billion, with a fixed-price option for additional air defense worth NOK 2.5 billion.</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>This is being done to:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Strengthen Norway's air defense against attacks.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Replace equipment donated to Ukraine.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Facilitate future donations.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Double the number of air defense batteries in Norway.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>The agreement includes:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Ordering more NASAMS launchers and fire control units.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Pre-ordering long-lead components.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>An option to buy more air defense in the future.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Benefits of the new contract:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Faster delivery of air defense systems to the Armed Forces.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Increased production capacity in the Norwegian defense industry.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Norway gains access to the latest generation of NASAMS.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Norway strengthens its ability to defend itself against air threats.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>This is a historic investment in Norwegian air defense. It will help make Norway safer and better equipped to meet future threats.</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Key points:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>The contract is an extension of an earlier agreement between the Norwegian Defence Materiel Agency (NDMA) and KDA.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>The agreement ensures that Norway will have quick access to long-lead NASAMS components.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>KDA has invested in expanding its production capacity to meet the increased demand.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Norway is taking a leading role in the development of air defense technology.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Additional details:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>The new NASAMS systems will be delivered to the Norwegian Armed Forces over the next few years.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>The contract also includes an option for Norway to buy additional NASAMS systems in the future.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>This investment is part of Norway's ongoing efforts to modernize its armed forces.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->
