<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Acquisition Costs</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Configuration</th><th>Cost (EUR)</th><th>Cost (USD)</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>SAMP/T battery (standard)</strong></td><td>€500-700 million</td><td>$550-770M</td><td>With Arabel radar</td></tr><tr><td><strong>SAMP/T NG battery</strong></td><td>€700-900 million</td><td>$770M-1B</td><td>With Ground Fire 300 radar</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Ground Fire 300 radar</strong></td><td>€150-200 million</td><td>$165-220M</td><td>New AESA radar for NG</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Arabel radar</strong></td><td>€80-120 million</td><td>$88-130M</td><td>Original radar</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Launcher (8 cells)</strong></td><td>€25-35 million</td><td>$27-38M</td><td>Vertical launch</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Interceptor Costs</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Variant</th><th>Cost per Missile (EUR)</th><th>Cost (USD)</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Aster 30 B1NT</strong></td><td>€2.5-3.5 million</td><td>$2.8-3.8M</td><td>Enhanced TBM capability</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Aster 30 B1</strong></td><td>€2-2.5 million</td><td>$2.2-2.8M</td><td>Standard long-range</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Aster 30</strong></td><td>€1.5-2 million</td><td>$1.7-2.2M</td><td>Original version</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Aster 15</strong></td><td>€1-1.5 million</td><td>$1.1-1.7M</td><td>Naval/short-range</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Known Procurements</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Buyer</th><th>Year</th><th>Value</th><th>Contents</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Singapore</strong></td><td>2020</td><td>~$1.1 billion</td><td>SAMP/T systems (quantity unclear)</td></tr><tr><td><strong>France/Italy (upgrade)</strong></td><td>2023</td><td>€700 million+</td><td>SAMP/T NG conversion</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">SAMP/T vs. Patriot Cost Comparison</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Factor</th><th>SAMP/T NG</th><th>Patriot PAC-3 MSE</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Battery acquisition</strong></td><td>€700-900M</td><td>€900M-1.1B</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Interceptor (primary)</strong></td><td>€2.5-3.5M</td><td>€4-5M</td></tr><tr><td><strong>European industrial content</strong></td><td>100%</td><td>~10-15%</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Offset potential</strong></td><td>High</td><td>Limited</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Competition</strong></td><td>Single source</td><td>Single source</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>SAMP/T offers lower per-unit costs, but Patriot’s larger production base may provide better long-term availability and upgrade paths.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">David’s Sling</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Manufacturer:</strong> Rafael Advanced Defense Systems (Israel) / Raytheon (USA)<br /><strong>Primary operators:</strong> Israel, Finland (on order)<br /><strong>In service since:</strong> 2017</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Acquisition Costs</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Component</th><th>Cost (USD)</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Battery</strong></td><td>$500-700 million</td><td>With MMR radar</td></tr><tr><td><strong>MMR radar</strong></td><td>$100-150 million</td><td>Multi-mission radar</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Launcher</strong></td><td>$30-40 million</td><td>12 missiles per launcher</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Interceptor Costs</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Variant</th><th>Cost per Missile</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Stunner</strong></td><td>$1-1.5 million</td><td>Primary interceptor</td></tr><tr><td><strong>SkyCeptor</strong></td><td>$500K-1 million</td><td>Cost-reduced variant for export</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>David’s Sling’s interceptor cost advantage is significant: <strong>Stunner costs roughly 75% less than PAC-3 MSE</strong> while addressing similar threat classes (cruise missiles, large rockets, short-range ballistic missiles).</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Finland Procurement</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Finland’s 2023 David’s Sling selection provides recent export pricing data:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Element</th><th>Value</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Contract value</strong></td><td>~$400-500 million (estimated)</td><td>Finnish sources</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Scope</strong></td><td>1 battery + interceptors</td><td>Initial capability</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Delivery</strong></td><td>2026-2028</td><td>Projected</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Medium-Range Air Defense Systems</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The volume tier of air defense, these systems provide cost-effective coverage against aircraft, cruise missiles, and drones without the acquisition and sustainment burden of long-range systems.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">NASAMS</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Manufacturer:</strong> Kongsberg Defence &amp; Aerospace (Norway) / Raytheon (USA)<br /><strong>Primary operators:</strong> Norway, USA, Finland, Netherlands, Spain, Lithuania, Chile, Indonesia, Qatar, Ukraine, and others<br /><strong>In service since:</strong> 1994 (NASAMS I); 2019 (NASAMS 3)</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Acquisition Costs</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Configuration</th><th>Cost (USD)</th><th>Cost (NOK)</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>NASAMS 3 battery (standard)</strong></td><td>$250-350 million</td><td>2.7-3.8 billion</td><td>3-4 launchers, radar, C2</td></tr><tr><td><strong>NASAMS 2 battery</strong></td><td>$150-250 million</td><td>1.6-2.7 billion</td><td>Previous generation</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Fire Distribution Center</strong></td><td>$30-50 million</td><td>325-540 million</td><td>C2 node</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Launcher (6 cells)</strong></td><td>$15-25 million</td><td>160-270 million</td><td>Canister-based</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Sentinel radar</strong></td><td>$25-40 million</td><td>270-430 million</td><td>3D surveillance</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Interceptor Costs</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Variant</th><th>Cost per Missile</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>AMRAAM-ER</strong></td><td>$1.5-2.5 million</td><td>Extended range (40+ km)</td></tr><tr><td><strong>AIM-120C AMRAAM</strong></td><td>$1-1.5 million</td><td>Standard variant</td></tr><tr><td><strong>AIM-9X Sidewinder</strong></td><td>$500K-800K</td><td>Short-range option</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Recent Procurements</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Buyer</th><th>Year</th><th>Value</th><th>Contents</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Norway</strong></td><td>2024</td><td>NOK 6.5 billion (~$600M)</td><td>Up to 4 batteries, NASAMS upgrade</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Ukraine</strong></td><td>2022-2024</td><td>$1.2 billion+</td><td>8+ systems donated/purchased</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Australia</strong></td><td>2021</td><td>AUD 900M (~$600M)</td><td>NASAMS 3 for short-range air defense</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Hungary</strong></td><td>2020</td><td>$1 billion</td><td>NASAMS + radar integration</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Norway’s NASAMS Investment</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>As the co-developer and anchor customer, Norway’s NASAMS costs provide baseline reference:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Investment Phase</th><th>Value (NOK)</th><th>Value (USD)</th><th>Scope</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>2024 contract</strong></td><td>6.5 billion</td><td>~$600 million</td><td>Up to 4 batteries equipment</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Cumulative (est.)</strong></td><td>15+ billion</td><td>~$1.4 billion</td><td>Total Norwegian NASAMS investment</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">IRIS-T SLM</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Manufacturer:</strong> Diehl Defence (Germany)<br /><strong>Primary operators:</strong> Germany, Egypt, Ukraine, Slovenia, Estonia (on order), and others<br /><strong>In service since:</strong> 2022 (SLM variant)</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Acquisition Costs</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Configuration</th><th>Cost (EUR)</th><th>Cost (USD)</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>IRIS-T SLM battery</strong></td><td>€150-200 million</td><td>$165-220M</td><td>3 launchers, radar, C2</td></tr><tr><td><strong>TRML-4D radar</strong></td><td>€40-60 million</td><td>$44-66M</td><td>Multi-function AESA</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Launcher (8 cells)</strong></td><td>€20-30 million</td><td>$22-33M</td><td>Vertical launch</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Interceptor Costs</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Variant</th><th>Cost per Missile (EUR)</th><th>Cost (USD)</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>IRIS-T SL</strong></td><td>€380-450K</td><td>$420-500K</td><td>40 km range</td></tr><tr><td><strong>IRIS-T SLS</strong></td><td>€250-350K</td><td>$275-385K</td><td>12 km range (SHORAD)</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>IRIS-T’s interceptor cost is the lowest among medium-range Western systems—<strong>roughly 60-70% less than AMRAAM</strong>—making it economically attractive for high-volume engagements.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Recent Procurements</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Buyer</th><th>Year</th><th>Value</th><th>Contents</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Germany (domestic)</strong></td><td>2022-2025</td><td>€4+ billion</td><td>Multiple batteries + stockpile</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Ukraine</strong></td><td>2022-2024</td><td>€1.5+ billion</td><td>4+ systems (donated/funded)</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Egypt</strong></td><td>2021</td><td>€800+ million</td><td>Multiple batteries</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Estonia</strong></td><td>2023</td><td>€300+ million</td><td>Battery with integration</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Slovenia</strong></td><td>2024</td><td>€300+ million</td><td>IRIS-T SLM battery</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Sky Sabre / CAMM</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Manufacturer:</strong> MBDA<br /><strong>Primary operators:</strong> UK, Sweden (as RBS 98), Poland (on order)<br /><strong>In service since:</strong> 2021</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Acquisition Costs</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Configuration</th><th>Cost (GBP)</th><th>Cost (USD)</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Sky Sabre battery</strong></td><td>£200-280 million</td><td>$250-350M</td><td>UK configuration</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Giraffe AMB radar</strong></td><td>£40-60 million</td><td>$50-75M</td><td>Saab radar</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Launcher (8 cells)</strong></td><td>£15-25 million</td><td>$19-31M</td><td>Soft-launch</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Interceptor Costs</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Variant</th><th>Cost (GBP)</th><th>Cost (USD)</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>CAMM</strong></td><td>£800K-1.2M</td><td>$1-1.5M</td><td>25+ km range</td></tr><tr><td><strong>CAMM-ER</strong></td><td>£1.2-1.8M</td><td>$1.5-2.3M</td><td>45+ km range</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Poland CAMM Procurement</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Poland’s Narew (short-range) program selected CAMM:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Element</th><th>Value</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Total program</strong></td><td>$2-3 billion (estimated)</td><td>Multiple phases</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Phase I</strong></td><td>~$800 million</td><td>Initial batteries</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Integration</strong></td><td>Significant</td><td>With Polish radars</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Short-Range and Counter-Drone Systems</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The fastest-growing segment of air defense investment, driven by drone proliferation and the economics of engaging low-cost threats.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Iron Dome</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Manufacturer:</strong> Rafael Advanced Defense Systems (Israel)<br /><strong>Primary operators:</strong> Israel, USA (Marine Corps)<br /><strong>In service since:</strong> 2011</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Acquisition Costs</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Component</th><th>Cost (USD)</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Battery</strong></td><td>$50-100 million</td><td>3-4 launchers, radar shared</td></tr><tr><td><strong>EL/M-2084 radar</strong></td><td>$50 million+</td><td>Shared across multiple batteries</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Launcher (20 cells)</strong></td><td>$5-10 million</td><td>Rapid reload capable</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Interceptor Costs</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Variant</th><th>Cost per Missile</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Tamir</strong></td><td>$40-50K</td><td>Primary interceptor</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Tamir NG</strong></td><td>$50-60K (estimated)</td><td>Enhanced seeker</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Tamir’s low cost enables engagement of threats that would be economically prohibitive for other systems. However, even at $40K, engaging a $500 drone creates 80:1 cost disadvantage.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":4} -->
<h4 class="wp-block-heading">US Iron Dome</h4>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The US procurement provides export pricing data:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Element</th><th>Value</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Initial batteries</strong></td><td>$373 million</td><td>2 batteries for Marine Corps</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Per-battery cost</strong></td><td>~$186 million</td><td>Higher than Israeli domestic</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Integration costs</strong></td><td>Significant</td><td>US C2 integration</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Directed Energy / Laser Systems</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Laser systems promise near-zero marginal cost per engagement, fundamentally changing air defense economics.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>System</th><th>Developer</th><th>Estimated Cost</th><th>Cost per Shot</th><th>Status</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Iron Beam</strong></td><td>Rafael</td><td>$50-100M per system</td><td>~$2</td><td>Development</td></tr><tr><td><strong>HELWS</strong></td><td>Raytheon</td><td>$15-25M per system</td><td>~$1</td><td>Limited fielding</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Dragonfire</strong></td><td>UK consortium</td><td>£100M+ program</td><td>TBD</td><td>Testing</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Rheinmetall HEL</strong></td><td>Germany</td><td>€30-50M per system</td><td>~$1</td><td>Development</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>The economic case:</strong> A laser system costing $50 million that can engage 1,000 drones at $2 each ($2,000 total) vs. a missile system where 1,000 engagements would cost $40-50 million (using Tamir) fundamentally changes procurement calculus.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Cost Trend Analysis</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Interceptor Cost Trajectories (2015-2025)</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>System</th><th>2015 Cost</th><th>2025 Cost</th><th>Change</th><th>Driver</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>PAC-3 MSE</strong></td><td>$3.5M</td><td>$5M+</td><td>+43%</td><td>Inflation, supply chain</td></tr><tr><td><strong>SM-3 Block IIA</strong></td><td>$24M</td><td>$33M</td><td>+38%</td><td>Limited production</td></tr><tr><td><strong>AMRAAM</strong></td><td>$800K</td><td>$1.3M</td><td>+63%</td><td>Demand surge</td></tr><tr><td><strong>IRIS-T SL</strong></td><td>€350K</td><td>€430K</td><td>+23%</td><td>Modest increase</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Tamir</strong></td><td>$35K</td><td>$50K</td><td>+43%</td><td>Production scaling</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Key trends:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>US-origin interceptors show highest inflation (40-60%)</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>European interceptors more price-stable</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Demand from Ukraine has accelerated price increases across all systems</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Production capacity, not technology, is the primary cost driver</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Production Capacity Constraints</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Current production rates vs. wartime consumption estimates:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Interceptor</th><th>Annual Production</th><th>Days of High-Intensity Combat</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>PAC-3 MSE</strong></td><td>500-650 missiles</td><td>10-15 days</td></tr><tr><td><strong>IRIS-T SL</strong></td><td>300-400 missiles</td><td>8-12 days</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Tamir</strong></td><td>1,000+ missiles</td><td>2-3 days</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>This production/consumption mismatch explains why interceptor costs are increasing despite economies of scale: production cannot keep pace with demand, eliminating competitive pricing pressure.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Total Cost of Ownership: 20-Year Scenarios</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>For strategic planners, these scenarios estimate total cost of ownership for representative national architectures.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Scenario A: Small Nation (Norway-scale)</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Component</th><th>Units</th><th>Acquisition</th><th>20-Year O&amp;M</th><th>20-Year Interceptors</th><th>Total</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Patriot PAC-3 MSE</td><td>2 batteries</td><td>$2.2B</td><td>$1.5B</td><td>$500M</td><td><strong>$4.2B</strong></td></tr><tr><td>NASAMS 3</td><td>4 batteries</td><td>$1.2B</td><td>$600M</td><td>$200M</td><td><strong>$2.0B</strong></td></tr><tr><td>SHORAD</td><td>TBD</td><td>$300M</td><td>$150M</td><td>$50M</td><td><strong>$500M</strong></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Total</strong></td><td>—</td><td>$3.7B</td><td>$2.25B</td><td>$750M</td><td><strong>$6.7B</strong></td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Scenario B: Medium Nation (Poland-scale)</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Component</th><th>Units</th><th>Acquisition</th><th>20-Year O&amp;M</th><th>20-Year Interceptors</th><th>Total</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Patriot PAC-3 MSE</td><td>6 batteries</td><td>$6.5B</td><td>$4B</td><td>$1.5B</td><td><strong>$12B</strong></td></tr><tr><td>NASAMS/Medium</td><td>8 batteries</td><td>$2.5B</td><td>$1.2B</td><td>$500M</td><td><strong>$4.2B</strong></td></tr><tr><td>SHORAD</td><td>Multiple</td><td>$800M</td><td>$400M</td><td>$200M</td><td><strong>$1.4B</strong></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Total</strong></td><td>—</td><td>$9.8B</td><td>$5.6B</td><td>$2.2B</td><td><strong>$17.6B</strong></td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Scenario C: Major European Power (Germany-scale)</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Component</th><th>Units</th><th>Acquisition</th><th>20-Year O&amp;M</th><th>20-Year Interceptors</th><th>Total</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Arrow-3</td><td>2-3 batteries</td><td>$5B</td><td>$2B</td><td>$500M</td><td><strong>$7.5B</strong></td></tr><tr><td>Patriot PAC-3 MSE</td><td>12 batteries</td><td>$12B</td><td>$7B</td><td>$3B</td><td><strong>$22B</strong></td></tr><tr><td>IRIS-T SLM</td><td>12 batteries</td><td>$2.5B</td><td>$1.2B</td><td>$600M</td><td><strong>$4.3B</strong></td></tr><tr><td>SHORAD/C-UAS</td><td>Extensive</td><td>$2B</td><td>$1B</td><td>$300M</td><td><strong>$3.3B</strong></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Total</strong></td><td>—</td><td>$21.5B</td><td>$11.2B</td><td>$4.4B</td><td><strong>$37.1B</strong></td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Data Sources and Methodology</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Primary Sources</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Government Sources:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>US Defense Security Cooperation Agency (DSCA) FMS notifications</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Congressional Budget Office reports</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>European Defence Agency publications</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>National defense budget documents (Norway, Germany, Poland, UK, France)</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Manufacturer Disclosures:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Annual reports (Raytheon RTX, Lockheed Martin, MBDA, Kongsberg, Diehl)</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Investor presentations</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Contract announcements</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Research Institutions:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>CSIS Missile Defense Project</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>IISS Military Balance</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>SIPRI Arms Transfers Database</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>RAND Corporation studies</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Limitations</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>This database should be used with the following caveats:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list {"ordered":true} -->
<ol class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>All figures are estimates.</strong> Actual contract values often remain classified or commercially sensitive.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Configuration matters enormously.</strong> A “battery” can mean different things across systems and buyers.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>FMS overhead.</strong> US Foreign Military Sales adds 15-20% to base costs; our figures attempt to normalize this but cannot always succeed.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Currency volatility.</strong> EUR/USD fluctuations of 10-15% can significantly affect comparisons.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Offset distortions.</strong> Industrial offset requirements can add 10-30% to apparent costs while providing domestic economic benefits.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Time value.</strong> Contracts span multiple years; we use announcement-year values.</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ol>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Revision History</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Date</th><th>Changes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>January 2026</strong></td><td>Initial publication; comprehensive database structure</td></tr><tr><td><em>Planned Q2 2026</em></td><td>Update with Norway Patriot decision data (if available)</td></tr><tr><td><em>Planned Q3 2026</em></td><td>Annual interceptor cost refresh</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><em>This database is maintained as a public resource. Corrections, additional data points, and sourced updates are welcome.</em></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Related articles:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><a href="/air-defense-systems-comparison-matrix/">The Complete Air Defense Systems Comparison Matrix</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><a href="/2025/03/18/patriot-vs-samp-t-air-and-missile-defense-systems-comparison/">Patriot vs. SAMP/T: Air and Missile Defense Systems Comparison</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><a href="/2024/04/06/what-does-the-patriot-missile-system-cost/">What Does the Patriot Air and Missile Defense System Cost?</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><a href="/2025/06/26/the-drone-defense-economics-crisis-when-3m-missiles-target-38k-drones/">The Drone Defense Economics Crisis</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

---

## Counter-Drone Defense Economics: Why Europe Needs a New Approach to C-UAS

**Date:** Sat, 03 Jan 2026 00:06:46 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2026/01/03/counter-drone-defense-economics-why-europe-needs-a-new-approach-to-c-uas/](http://norskluftvern.com/2026/01/03/counter-drone-defense-economics-why-europe-needs-a-new-approach-to-c-uas/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><em>The proliferation of cheap drones has broken the traditional air defense cost model. Europe’s response so far has been fragmented and inadequate. This analysis examines the C-UAS challenge, surveys available solutions, and asks what a coherent European—and Norwegian—approach might look like.</em></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">The $3 Million Problem</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>In March 2024, a Patriot battery in Ukraine engaged a Russian Shahed-136 drone. The intercept was successful. The math was not.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The Shahed costs Iran approximately $20,000-50,000 to produce. The PAC-2 GEM-T missile that destroyed it costs $1-2 million. Even using the most conservative estimates, the defender spent 20-100 times more than the attacker.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>This is not an edge case. It is the new normal.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Ukraine fires an estimated 10,000+ air defense missiles annually. If even 20% of engagements involve this kind of cost asymmetry, the economic sustainability of traditional air defense becomes questionable within years, not decades.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The Houthis demonstrated the same principle against the US Navy in the Red Sea throughout 2024. USS Carney and other destroyers expended SM-2 missiles ($2.1 million each) and Evolved Sea Sparrow Missiles ($1.65 million) against drone swarms costing a fraction of the defensive response. The USS Dwight D. Eisenhower carrier strike group reportedly fired over 500 munitions in a six-month deployment—a significant portion of which were expensive missiles engaging cheap threats.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>This cost inversion represents a fundamental challenge to how NATO nations have structured air defense for decades. The traditional model assumed expensive platforms (aircraft, cruise missiles) as primary threats, justifying expensive interceptors. Drones have collapsed that assumption.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Defining the C-UAS Challenge</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Counter-Unmanned Aerial Systems (C-UAS) encompasses technologies designed to detect, track, identify, and neutralize drone threats. The challenge is not singular—it spans a threat spectrum that demands different solutions at different price points.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">The Drone Threat Taxonomy</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>NATO categorizes UAS by size and capability:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Group</th><th>Weight</th><th>Example</th><th>Typical Cost</th><th>Threat Profile</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Group 1</strong></td><td>&lt;9 kg</td><td>DJI Mavic, FPV drones</td><td>$500-5,000</td><td>Reconnaissance, grenade delivery</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Group 2</strong></td><td>9-25 kg</td><td>Commercial/military small UAS</td><td>$5,000-50,000</td><td>ISR, small munitions</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Group 3</strong></td><td>25-600 kg</td><td>Shahed-136, Bayraktar TB2</td><td>$20,000-5M</td><td>Strike, persistent ISR</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Group 4</strong></td><td>&gt;600 kg</td><td>MQ-9 Reaper class</td><td>$10M+</td><td>Strategic ISR, precision strike</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Traditional air defense systems (Patriot, SAMP/T, NASAMS) were designed primarily for Group 4 threats and aircraft. They can engage Group 3 drones but at economically unsustainable cost ratios. For Groups 1-2, they are largely ineffective—these targets are too small, too slow, and too cheap to justify engagement.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The C-UAS problem is therefore not about capability but about economics: how do you defend against threats that cost 1/100th to 1/1000th of your interceptors?</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Why Traditional Air Defense Struggles</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Factor</th><th>Traditional GBAD</th><th>C-UAS Requirement</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Target RCS</strong></td><td>&gt;1 m² (aircraft)</td><td>0.001-0.1 m² (small drones)</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Target speed</strong></td><td>200-3,000 km/h</td><td>50-200 km/h</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Target altitude</strong></td><td>100-30,000 m</td><td>0-500 m (often)</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Engagement cost</strong></td><td>$500K-5M acceptable</td><td>Must be &lt;$100K, ideally &lt;$10K</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Magazine depth</strong></td><td>4-16 missiles per launcher</td><td>Potentially hundreds of engagements per day</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Detection range</strong></td><td>Optimized for high/fast</td><td>Struggles with low/slow/small</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>NASAMS and IRIS-T SLM can engage larger drones effectively, but using a $400,000-1.5 million missile against a $20,000 Shahed still represents a 20:1 cost disadvantage. Against a $500 FPV drone, the ratio becomes absurd.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">The C-UAS Solution Landscape</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The market has responded to the drone threat with a proliferation of counter-drone technologies. These fall into several categories, each with distinct cost profiles and effectiveness characteristics.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Detection and Tracking Systems</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Before you can defeat a drone, you must find it. Detection technologies include:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Radar Systems</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Traditional air defense radars often lack sensitivity for small UAS</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Specialized C-UAS radars (like Raytheon’s KuRFS) optimize for low-RCS targets</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Cost: $5-50 million depending on capability</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Limitation: Struggle with ground clutter in complex terrain</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>RF Detection</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Passive systems that detect drone-to-controller communications</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Can identify drone type and locate both drone and operator</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Cost: $100K-2M per system</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Limitation: Ineffective against autonomous drones with no RF emissions</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Electro-Optical/Infrared</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Cameras and thermal sensors for visual detection and tracking</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Effective for final engagement cueing</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Cost: $50K-500K per system</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Limitation: Weather-dependent, limited range</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Acoustic Sensors</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Detect drone motor/propeller signatures</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Low cost, passive operation</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Cost: $10K-100K per sensor</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Limitation: Very short range, environmental noise interference</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Effective C-UAS requires <strong>sensor fusion</strong>—combining multiple detection methods to overcome individual limitations. This integration challenge is often more difficult than the individual sensor technologies.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Soft-Kill Effectors (Non-Kinetic)</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Non-kinetic solutions disable drones without physical destruction, offering potentially unlimited “magazine depth.”</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>RF Jamming</strong><br>Systems that overwhelm drone control links, forcing the drone to land, return home, or lose control.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>System</th><th>Manufacturer</th><th>Type</th><th>Range</th><th>Cost</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>DroneGun Tactical</td><td>DroneShield</td><td>Handheld</td><td>1-2 km</td><td>$30-50K</td></tr><tr><td>AUDS</td><td>Chess Dynamics</td><td>Fixed/mobile</td><td>5+ km</td><td>$1-2M</td></tr><tr><td>DedroneDefender</td><td>Dedrone</td><td>Fixed</td><td>1-2 km</td><td>$200-500K</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Limitations:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Ineffective against autonomous/pre-programmed drones</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Can jam friendly communications</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Legal restrictions in many jurisdictions</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Drones increasingly use jam-resistant protocols</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>GPS Spoofing</strong><br>Systems that feed false navigation data to drones, redirecting them away from protected areas or forcing landing.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Limitations:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Can affect other GPS-dependent systems</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Sophisticated drones use inertial navigation backup</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Legal and safety concerns near airports</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>High-Power Microwave (HPM)</strong><br>Directed energy systems that fry drone electronics.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>System</th><th>Developer</th><th>Status</th><th>Range</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Leonidas</td><td>Epirus (US)</td><td>Fielded</td><td>1+ km</td><td>Counter-swarm capable</td></tr><tr><td>RAVEN</td><td>Raytheon</td><td>Development</td><td>TBD</td><td>Counter-electronics</td></tr><tr><td>Mjölnir</td><td>AFRL (US)</td><td>Development</td><td>TBD</td><td>High-power prototype</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>HPM systems offer the theoretical advantage of engaging swarms simultaneously and very low cost-per-shot. However, they remain largely developmental and face challenges with range and precision targeting.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Hard-Kill Effectors (Kinetic)</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>When soft-kill fails or isn’t appropriate, kinetic solutions physically destroy the target.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Gun-Based Systems</strong><br>Cannons with specialized ammunition for drone engagement.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>System</th><th>Country</th><th>Caliber</th><th>Range</th><th>Cost per System</th><th>Cost per Engagement</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Skynex</td><td>Germany</td><td>35mm</td><td>4 km</td><td>€30-50M</td><td>~$1,000</td></tr><tr><td>Phalanx (C-RAM)</td><td>USA</td><td>20mm</td><td>2 km</td><td>$15M</td><td>~$100</td></tr><tr><td>Skyranger 30</td><td>Germany</td><td>30mm</td><td>3 km</td><td>€15-25M</td><td>~$500</td></tr><tr><td>Oerlikon Revolver</td><td>Switzerland</td><td>35mm</td><td>4 km</td><td>€20-30M</td><td>~$1,000</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Gun systems offer excellent cost-per-engagement economics but limited range. They’re ideal for point defense but cannot provide wide-area coverage.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Specialized C-UAS Interceptors</strong><br>Purpose-built missiles and drones designed to kill drones cost-effectively.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>System</th><th>Country</th><th>Type</th><th>Range</th><th>Unit Cost</th><th>Cost per Kill</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Coyote Block 2</td><td>USA</td><td>Jet-powered interceptor</td><td>10-15 km</td><td>$100K</td><td>$100K</td></tr><tr><td>MADIS/Stinger</td><td>USA</td><td>IR missile</td><td>8 km</td><td>$120K</td><td>$120K</td></tr><tr><td>Roadrunner-M</td><td>USA (Anduril)</td><td>Reusable interceptor</td><td>TBD</td><td>$200-300K</td><td>Lower if recovered</td></tr><tr><td>Anvil</td><td>USA (Anduril)</td><td>Kamikaze interceptor</td><td>Short</td><td>$10-20K</td><td>$10-20K</td></tr><tr><td>SkyWall 300</td><td>UK</td><td>Net launcher</td><td>300m</td><td>$50-100K</td><td>$100-500</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Directed Energy (Laser) Systems</strong><br>The theoretical endgame for C-UAS economics: near-zero marginal cost per engagement.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>System</th><th>Developer</th><th>Power</th><th>Status</th><th>Expected Cost per Shot</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Iron Beam</td><td>Rafael (Israel)</td><td>100 kW</td><td>Near operational</td><td>~$2</td></tr><tr><td>HELWS</td><td>Raytheon (US)</td><td>50 kW</td><td>Limited fielding</td><td>~$1</td></tr><tr><td>Dragonfire</td><td>UK consortium</td><td>50 kW</td><td>Testing</td><td>TBD</td></tr><tr><td>Rheinmetall HEL</td><td>Germany</td><td>20-100 kW</td><td>Development</td><td>~$1</td></tr><tr><td>HELMA-P</td><td>EU</td><td>100 kW</td><td>Development</td><td>TBD</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Lasers promise to solve the cost asymmetry problem entirely. A $50 million laser system that can engage 1,000 drones at $2 each ($2,000 total) versus a missile system where 1,000 engagements cost $100 million represents a fundamental shift in defensive economics.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>However, current laser systems face limitations:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Weather dependence</strong>: Rain, fog, dust degrade effectiveness</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Dwell time</strong>: Must hold on target for seconds to achieve kill</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Power requirements</strong>: High-power systems need substantial electrical generation</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Range limitations</strong>: Effective range currently 1-7 km for most systems</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Maturity</strong>: Most systems remain developmental or in limited fielding</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Operational C-UAS: What’s Actually Deployed</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Theory and marketing differ from operational reality. Which systems are actually defeating drones in combat?</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">United States</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The US Army has fielded multiple C-UAS systems in response to drone threats in the Middle East:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>M-LIDS (Mobile-Low, Slow, Small UAS Integrated Defeat System)</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Coyote Block 2 interceptors + KuRFS radar</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Mounted on M-ATV vehicles</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Actively engaging drones in Syria, Iraq, Jordan</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>FS-LIDS (Fixed Site-LIDS)</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Same components in fixed installation configuration</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Protecting bases and installations</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>M-SHORAD</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Stryker vehicles with Stinger missiles + 30mm cannon</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Combines traditional SHORAD with C-UAS capability</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The Army plans to procure <strong>6,700 Coyote interceptors</strong> from 2025-2029, indicating the scale of perceived need.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Israel</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Israel operates the world’s most combat-tested C-UAS architecture:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Iron Dome</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Originally designed for rockets/artillery, adapted for larger drones</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Tamir interceptor at $40-50K offers better economics than traditional SAMs</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Has engaged thousands of targets since 2011</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Iron Beam</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Laser system specifically for C-UAS and short-range rockets</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Expected operational 2025</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Intended to complement Iron Dome for cheapest threats</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Drone Dome</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Integrated detection and jamming system</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Deployed around critical infrastructure</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Ukraine</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The most intensive C-UAS laboratory in history:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Layered Defense</strong><br>Ukraine employs everything from Patriot (for high-value targets) through NASAMS and IRIS-T (medium threats) to mobile gun teams and electronic warfare (cheap drones). The key lesson: <strong>no single system suffices</strong>.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Improvised Solutions</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Pickup trucks with machine guns</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Modified AA guns from storage</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Civilian volunteers with shotguns</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Extensive EW networks</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Cost Pressure</strong><br>Ukraine has repeatedly emphasized the unsustainability of using $1M+ missiles against $50K drones, driving demand for cheaper solutions and gun-based systems.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Europe’s Fragmented Response</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Despite the obvious threat, European C-UAS procurement remains fragmented and underinvested relative to the challenge.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Germany</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Germany has made the most substantial C-UAS commitments:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Skynex</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>35mm gun-based system with AHEAD programmable ammunition</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Ordered for Bundeswehr</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Also selected by several export customers</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>IRIS-T SLS</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Short-range variant of IRIS-T for SHORAD/C-UAS</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Lower cost than SLM but still missile-based</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Rheinmetall HEL</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Laser development with multiple prototypes demonstrated</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Targeting operational capability by 2027</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Nächstbereichschutz</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Near-area protection program for laser C-UAS</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>€20-40 million range per system expected</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">United Kingdom</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Dragonfire</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>50 kW laser demonstrator</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Successfully tested 2024</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Potential operational capability by 2027</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Sky Sabre / CAMM</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Can engage larger drones but cost-prohibitive for swarms</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Counter-UAS Capability (CUAS)</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Evaluation program examining multiple solutions</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>No major procurement announcements yet</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">France</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Parade</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Multi-sensor C-UAS system</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Combines radar, EO, RF detection with jamming effectors</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Deployed for major events (Olympics 2024)</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>No Laser Program</strong><br>France has notably not announced a military laser C-UAS program, relying instead on traditional effectors and jamming.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Nordic Countries</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Finland</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Evaluating C-UAS as part of broader air defense modernization</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>No major procurement announced</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Sweden</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>SAAB developing C-UAS solutions</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>No major domestic procurement announced</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Denmark</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Recent major air defense investment focused on NASAMS/Patriot-class</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>C-UAS not publicly prioritized</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Norway</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>No announced C-UAS program</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>NASAMS modernization ongoing</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Patriot consideration focused on BMD, not C-UAS</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">The European Gap</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Compared to US and Israeli investments, European C-UAS remains:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Dimension</th><th>US/Israel</th><th>Europe</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Deployed systems</strong></td><td>Multiple, combat-tested</td><td>Limited, mostly developmental</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Laser programs</strong></td><td>Operational/near-operational</td><td>2027+ timelines</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Procurement scale</strong></td><td>Thousands of interceptors</td><td>Dozens to hundreds</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Doctrinal integration</strong></td><td>Mature</td><td>Emerging</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Industrial base</strong></td><td>Established</td><td>Fragmented</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">The Norwegian Question</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Norway faces a specific C-UAS calculus shaped by geography, threat environment, and existing capabilities.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Current State</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Norway’s ground-based air defense centers on NASAMS, with Patriot under consideration for long-range/BMD capability. Neither system is optimized for C-UAS:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>System</th><th>C-UAS Capability</th><th>Limitation</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>NASAMS</strong></td><td>Can engage Group 3+ drones</td><td>AMRAAM too expensive for Group 1-2</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Patriot (if acquired)</strong></td><td>Can engage Group 3-4 drones</td><td>Massive cost asymmetry</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Norway has <strong>no dedicated C-UAS capability</strong> in current force structure or announced procurement plans.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Threat Assessment</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Norway’s drone threat environment differs from Ukraine or the Middle East:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Lower Immediate Threat</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>No active conflict</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Limited hostile drone activity currently</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Primary threat is Russian reconnaissance/harassment</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Specific Vulnerabilities</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Critical infrastructure (oil/gas platforms, power grid) exposed</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Military bases, particularly in the north</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Naval vessels in littoral operations</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Border monitoring challenges</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Potential Escalation</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Any conflict involving NATO’s northern flank would involve extensive drone use</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Russia has demonstrated mass drone employment doctrine</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Norwegian geography (coastline, mountains) creates C-UAS challenges</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">What Would Norway Need?</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>A coherent Norwegian C-UAS architecture might include:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Tier 1: Point Defense</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Gun-based systems (Skynex-class) for bases and critical infrastructure</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Laser systems as they mature (post-2027)</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Estimated need: 10-20 systems</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Estimated cost: NOK 3-6 billion</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Tier 2: Mobile Protection</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Vehicle-mounted C-UAS for maneuver forces</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Similar to US M-SHORAD concept</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Could integrate with existing CV90 fleet</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Estimated need: 30-50 systems</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Estimated cost: NOK 2-4 billion</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Tier 3: Wide-Area Detection</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Specialized C-UAS radar network</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Integration with NASAMS sensors</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>RF detection for drone localization</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Estimated cost: NOK 1-2 billion</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Total Estimated Investment: NOK 6-12 billion</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>This represents a significant investment—roughly equivalent to 1-2 NASAMS batteries—for a capability that Norway currently lacks entirely.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Integration with Layered Defense</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>C-UAS cannot be considered in isolation. It must integrate with Norway’s broader air defense architecture:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:syntaxhighlighter/code -->
<pre class="wp-block-syntaxhighlighter-code">Threat Spectrum          Norwegian Response
─────────────────────────────────────────────
Ballistic missiles  →    Patriot (under consideration)
Aircraft/cruise     →    Patriot + NASAMS
Large drones        →    NASAMS (with limitations)
Medium drones       →    [GAP]
Small drones        →    [GAP]
FPV/micro drones    →    [GAP]</pre>
<!-- /wp:syntaxhighlighter/code -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The gaps at the lower end of the spectrum represent the C-UAS requirement. Filling these gaps requires either:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list {"ordered":true} -->
<ol class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Dedicated C-UAS procurement</strong> (as outlined above)</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Upgraded NASAMS</strong> with cheaper effectors (if developed)</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Allied C-UAS support</strong> (relying on US/allied systems in crisis)</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Accepting risk</strong> at the low end of the threat spectrum</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ol>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Option 4 has been the de facto Norwegian approach. Recent events suggest this may be increasingly untenable.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">ESSI and European C-UAS Coordination</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The European Sky Shield Initiative (ESSI) provides a potential framework for coordinated C-UAS procurement, though current focus remains on traditional air defense systems (Arrow-3, Patriot, IRIS-T).</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">ESSI Potential for C-UAS</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Opportunity</th><th>Status</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Joint procurement of C-UAS systems</td><td>Not currently addressed</td></tr><tr><td>Shared sensor networks</td><td>Under discussion</td></tr><tr><td>Common C2 architecture</td><td>In development</td></tr><tr><td>Coordinated laser development</td><td>Limited</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>ESSI could theoretically enable:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Economies of scale</strong> for C-UAS interceptor procurement</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Interoperability</strong> between national systems</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Shared development</strong> of European laser systems</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Coordinated radar coverage</strong> for drone detection</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>However, ESSI’s current priority is filling the more traditional air defense gaps (BMD, long-range). C-UAS may remain a national responsibility for the near term.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">NATO C-UAS Initiatives</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>NATO has recognized the C-UAS challenge through various initiatives:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Counter-UAS Architecture Study</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Examining alliance-wide requirements</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>No major procurement recommendations yet</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>US Forward Presence</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>US Army C-UAS systems in Europe (Germany, Poland)</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Could provide model for European procurement</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Technology Sharing</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>US Coyote approved for allied sales</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li>Potential for European procurement of proven US systems</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Recommendations</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">For European Planners</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:list {"ordered":true} -->
<ol class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Accept C-UAS as a distinct tier of air defense</strong>, not an afterthought to traditional GBAD procurement</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Accelerate laser development timelines</strong>—the economics only close with directed energy</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Procure gun-based interim solutions</strong> while lasers mature; the threat exists now</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Establish common C-UAS standards</strong> through ESSI or NATO to enable interoperability</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Invest in sensor networks</strong> first; detection is the foundation of any C-UAS architecture</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ol>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">For Norwegian Planners</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:list {"ordered":true} -->
<ol class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Include C-UAS in the next defense planning cycle</strong> as a distinct capability requirement</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Evaluate gun-based systems</strong> (Skynex, Skyranger) for critical infrastructure protection</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Monitor German laser development</strong> for potential future procurement</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Ensure NASAMS modernization</strong> includes improved small-target detection</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Consider C-UAS integration</strong> with any Patriot procurement</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><strong>Assess industrial opportunities</strong>—Kongsberg could develop C-UAS solutions leveraging NASAMS expertise</li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ol>
<!-- /wp:list -->

<!-- wp:heading {"level":3} -->
<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Cost-Benefit Reality</h3>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The cost of inaction is not zero. Consider:</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:table -->
<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><thead><tr><th>Scenario</th><th>Cost of Defense</th><th>Cost of No Defense</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Drone harassment of oil platform</td><td>NOK 100M (C-UAS system)</td><td>Production disruption, potential casualties</td></tr><tr><td>Drone ISR of military exercise</td><td>NOK 50M (mobile C-UAS)</td><td>Intelligence compromise</td></tr><tr><td>Mass drone attack on base</td><td>NOK 500M (layered C-UAS)</td><td>Base destruction, aircraft losses in billions</td></tr></tbody></table></figure>
<!-- /wp:table -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The asymmetry that makes drones attractive to attackers is the same asymmetry that makes C-UAS investment attractive to defenders—if procured at the right cost point.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Conclusion: The Economics Will Force the Issue</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The drone revolution in warfare is not a future scenario. It is happening now, in Ukraine, in the Middle East, and in gray-zone activities worldwide. The economics are inexorable: defenders must find ways to engage $500-50,000 threats without expending $500,000-5,000,000 interceptors.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Europe’s current approach—traditional air defense systems with some jamming capability—will become economically unsustainable against determined drone employment. The nations that invest now in gun systems, laser development, and specialized interceptors will be better positioned when the threat intensifies.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Norway faces a choice: continue treating C-UAS as someone else’s problem, or recognize it as an essential component of a complete air defense architecture. The gap in Norwegian capability is real. The question is whether it will be addressed proactively or in crisis.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The drone doesn’t care about your procurement timeline. It just needs to be cheaper than your missile.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><em>This analysis consolidates and updates previous coverage of counter-drone systems on this site. For detailed examination of specific systems, see our <a href="/2025/07/10/counter-drone-systems-comparison-c-uas-technology-assessment/">Counter-Drone Systems Comparison</a> and <a href="/2025/06/26/the-drone-defense-economics-crisis-when-3m-missiles-target-38k-drones/">Drone Defense Economics</a> articles.</em></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>Related articles:</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:list -->
<ul class="wp-block-list"><!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><a href="/air-defense-systems-comparison-matrix/">The Complete Air Defense Systems Comparison Matrix</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><a href="/air-defense-systems-cost-database/">Air Defense Systems Cost Database</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><a href="/2025/04/15/iris-t-sl-vs-nasams-air-defense-systems-comparison/">IRIS-T SL vs. NASAMS: Air Defense Systems Comparison</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item -->

<!-- wp:list-item -->
<li><a href="/2025/07/30/norways-air-defense-evolution-bolstering-nasams-while-pursuing-ballistic-missile-defense/">Norway’s Air Defense Evolution</a></li>
<!-- /wp:list-item --></ul>
<!-- /wp:list -->

---

## Norway’s New Reach — And the Question It Leaves Open

**Date:** Fri, 20 Feb 2026 19:33:43 +0000  
**Author:** norskluftvern  
**Type:** Post  
**Link:** [http://norskluftvern.com/2026/02/20/norways-new-reach-and-the-question-it-leaves-open/](http://norskluftvern.com/2026/02/20/norways-new-reach-and-the-question-it-leaves-open/)

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p><strong>On January 30, 2026, Norway signed a contract for its first land-based long-range precision fire capability. The Chunmoo deal answered one of the most pressing questions in Norwegian Army modernization. It also brought another into sharper focus.</strong></p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>On a Thursday morning at the end of January, Norway’s Defence Minister Tore O. Sandvik stood before cameras in Oslo and described the signing of a $922 million contract with South Korea’s Hanwha Aerospace as “one of the largest investments ever made for the Army.” The K239 Chunmoo multiple launch rocket system — 16 launchers, a large package of precision-guided munitions, and an integrated logistics framework — represents Norway’s entry into land-based deep strike. For a country whose ground forces have operated without organic long-range rocket artillery, it is a meaningful threshold.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The contract is the visible part of a broader NOK 19 billion (~$2 billion) long-range fires programme. Launchers are expected in 2028 and 2029. Missiles — including variants with a stated range of up to 500 kilometers — follow in 2030 and 2031. The Norwegian Defence Materiel Agency assessed the Chunmoo as the only system in the competition that met all requirements for performance, delivery speed, and cost. Competitors from KNDS (EuroPULS), Rheinmetall, and the American HIMARS were evaluated and found wanting on one or more criteria, with KNDS eliminated from the competition in June 2025 before the final selection.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The decision places Norway in a growing group of NATO members operating or procuring the Chunmoo. Poland has ordered nearly 300 systems and established a domestic missile production line; Estonia signed an expansion of its Chunmoo order in late December 2025. Hanwha has indicated that missiles for all European customers will be supplied from Poland, a supply chain arrangement that Norway cited as a factor in its evaluation.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The Chunmoo build-out fills a capability gap that Norwegian defence planners have acknowledged for years. Long-range precision fires allow a force to suppress, disrupt, or destroy an adversary’s launch assets, command nodes, and logistics chains before they can be brought to bear. The Ukraine conflict has provided a sustained, if costly, demonstration of why this matters.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>What the Chunmoo does not do — and what its procurement now sits alongside — is intercept incoming ballistic missiles.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:separator -->
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity" />
<!-- /wp:separator -->

<!-- wp:heading -->
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">The Ballistic Missile Gap in the LTDP</h2>
<!-- /wp:heading -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>Norway’s 2025–2036 Long-Term Defence Plan (LTDP), titled <em>The Norwegian Defence Pledge</em>, is an unusually candid document about what the country currently lacks. The plan commits NOK 600 billion over twelve years to a comprehensive force modernization effort spanning navy, army, air force, and infrastructure. It is explicit on two air defence priorities: expansion of NASAMS and acquisition of a system to defend against tactical ballistic missiles.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The NASAMS expansion is progressing. Norway ordered new launchers and Fire Distribution Centers in December 2023 in a NOK 1.4 billion contract with Kongsberg Defence &amp; Aerospace, partly to replace systems donated to Ukraine and partly to begin planned capacity growth. A further NOK 1 billion order for NASAMS components — command posts, communications nodes, and THOR radios — was confirmed in October 2025. The LTDP foresees four additional NASAMS batteries for the Air Force and Army, along with new radars. This is a system Norway knows intimately; it co-developed and operates it, and its combat record in Ukraine — with approximately 900 successful interceptions and a reported effectiveness rate of approximately 94% against cruise missiles and Shahed-type drones — has validated the underlying investment logic.</p>
<!-- /wp:paragraph -->

<!-- wp:paragraph -->
<p>The LTDP’s second air defence commitment is worded carefully.
